title: The uncertain fate of non-Russian prisoners of war captured by Ukrainian forces
subhed: Hundreds of foreign fighters held in captivity in Ukraine find themselves in legal limbo, as Moscow refuses to include them in prisoner exchanges.

Al Jazeera says Israeli strike kills journalist in Gaza
subhed: The Israeli military issued a statement that cameraman Ahmed Wishah was killed in a 'precise strike' on Saturday, alleging that he was a Hamas militant. Wishah's brother and fellow Al Jazeera journalist Mohammed had also been killed in April by Israeli shelling.

title: France issues red heatwave alert on Sunday for third of country, bans public alcohol consumption at music festival
subhed: Météo-France announced the highest heatwave warning for 35 of France's departments for Sunday, including the Paris region. On Saturday, the government said that alcohol consumption in public spaces during the annual Fête de la Musique would be banned in departments under this alert.

title: French teachers find it a growing challenge to inspire a love of reading among students
subhed: French literature teachers have lamented a growing crisis within their classrooms. They argue that the traditional, formal nature of certain exercises and the list of required classics are failing to foster a desire to read among students.

title: Is air conditioning essential for coping with heatwaves? France might be changing its mind on the issue
subhed: While more and more people are installing air conditioning, this technology is still seen by many as a misguided response to climate change. Some experts have challenged that argument as France faces unusually high temperatures.

subhed: Le Monde's Culture desk offers its selection of concerts taking place on Sunday, June 21, as part of the national Fête de la Musique, which has become a global phenomenon.

title: Ending the war with Iran comes at the cost of major US concessions
subhed: The memorandum of understanding, signed on the evening of June 17 by the presidents of both countries is intended to end hostilities and allow for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Nuclear negotiations will begin immediately afterward.

title: After US signals another review of its military presence in Europe, allies grapple with Washington's ambiguities
subhed: On June 18, the US defense secretary announced a 'review' of the distribution of American NATO forces on the continent. The decision has raised concerns, even though it is being presented as a simple 'adjustment,' a senior US defense official told Le Monde.
